title: pittosporum verticillatum
intro: Pittosporum verticillatum, also known as the Australian cheesewood or black haw, is a species of flowering plant in the family Pittosporaceae. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it effective in treating various health issues. The health benefits of Pittosporum verticillatum include its ability to lower fever, reduce inflammation, and alleviate respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
